questions.question:
Consider the following four statements (A - D) related to the common from Rana tigrina, and select the correct option stating which ones are true (T) and which ones are false (F). Statements:

 On dry land it would die due to lack of O_(2) if its mouth is forcibly kept closed for a few days.
 It has four chambered heart.
 On dry land it turns uricotelic from ureotelic.
 Its life history is carried out in pond water.
questions.question_html:
Consider the following four statements (A - D) related to the common from Rana tigrina, and select the correct option stating which ones are true (T) and which ones are false (F). Statements:

  1. On dry land it would die due to lack of O2 if its mouth is forcibly kept closed for a few days.
  2. It has four chambered heart.
  3. On dry land it turns uricotelic from ureotelic.
  4. Its life history is carried out in pond water.

options.text: Testes $\rightarrow$ Vasa efferentia $\rightarrow$ Kidney $\rightarrow$ Seminal uesicle $\rightarrow$ Urinogenital duct $\rightarrow$ Cloaca
Testes $\rightarrow$ Bidder’s canal $\rightarrow$ Kidney $\rightarrow$ Vasa efferentia $\rightarrow$ Urinogenital duct $\rightarrow$ Cloaca
Testes $\rightarrow$ Vasa efferentia $\rightarrow$ Kidney $\rightarrow$ Bidder’s canal $\rightarrow$ Urinogenrital duct $\rightarrow$ Cloaca
Testes $\rightarrow$ Vasa efferentia $\rightarrow$ Bidder’s canal $\rightarrow$ Ureter $\rightarrow$ Cloaca

questions.question: Which of the following statement is not true regarding the reproductive system of the frog?
questions.question_html: Which of the following statement is not true regarding the reproductive system of the frog?

options.text: Functional connection present between kidneys and ovaries, oviduct arising from the ovaries opens into the cloaca with ureters.
options.html: Functional connection present between kidneys and ovaries, oviduct arising from the ovaries opens into the cloaca with ureters.

options.text: In the male frogs, two ureters emerge from the kidneys and ureters act as a urinogenital duct which opens into the cloaca.
options.html: In the male frogs, two ureters emerge from the kidneys and ureters act as a urinogenital duct which opens into the cloaca.

options.text: Vasa efferentia arises from testes and enters the kidneys on their side and opens into Bidder’s canal.
options.html: Vasa efferentia arises from testes and enters the kidneys on their side and opens into Bidder’s canal.

options.text: A mature female can lay 2500 to 3000 ova at a time.
options.html: A mature female can lay 2500 to 3000 ova at a time.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement are true about Rana tigrina?

 The most common species of frog found in India is Rana tigrina.
 In peak summer frog takes shelter in deep burrows to protect them from extreme heat, this is called hibernation.
 In peak winter frog takes shelter in deep burrows to protect them from extreme cold, this is called aestivation.
 The frog never drinks water but absorb it through the skin.
 Male frogs can be distinguished by the presence of vocal sacs and a copulatory pad on the first digit of the hind limbs.
questions.question_html:
Which of the following statement are true about Rana tigrina?

  1. The most common species of frog found in India is Rana tigrina.
  2. In peak summer frog takes shelter in deep burrows to protect them from extreme heat, this is called hibernation.
  3. In peak winter frog takes shelter in deep burrows to protect them from extreme cold, this is called aestivation.
  4. The frog never drinks water but absorb it through the skin.
  5. Male frogs can be distinguished by the presence of vocal sacs and a copulatory pad on the first digit of the hind limbs.

options.text: Male frog - (i), Testes (ii) - Adrenal gland (iii) Fat bodies (iv) Cloaca

questions.question:
Match the followings and choose the correct answer:

 
 
 (A) Touch
 (i) Nasal epithelium
 
 
 (B) Smell

 (C) Cranial nerves
 (iii) Sensory papillae
 
 
 (D) Medulla oblongata
 (iv) Brain
